per_stop.quote: We had a refreshing cold shower when we arrived. You pulled the chain & the water came down until you stopped pulling.
Eve swam out into the lake before breakfast. After breakfast John & Charles paddled/rowed right across the lake.
John said "Oh yes, Clung: I've seen him in 2 films." and that about summed up John's + Charles' interest in the Abbey.
lots of Dutch people who are very pleasant to camp with. lots of wooden cabins in the bushes.
There was an amusing picture on a restaurant window of St. George sharing a bottle of wine, seated at a table, with a dragon, + the dragon was gobbing the chicken between them.
Eve had to drive because Simon only had his dark glasses + it was dark. It seemed a long way with lots of roundabouts, + we were all glad when we got back safely.
Then we climbed up the amazing hill to the chapel. The "hill" is really the plug of an old volcano that has eroded away.
A fantastic view of the setting sun across the lake.
Rivers inside the tent. Wet sleeping bags.
Simon got cross at all the gift shops.
There was an amazing view from the ridge.
We went alongside the canal taking water to the power station at Madières. It was like a Madeira Levada.
The rough land is actually quite nice, next to a vineyard, more peaceful than the crowded site.
John didn't like his first course sea snails. After supper the boys went on dodgems.
Marble Streets. I like the city.
Charles pointed out beavers, Castor, to Eve.
Simon remarked that the trip is easy until you capsize then you feel the power of the water.
Beautiful vineyards all around on the hill slopes & from the van we could see the large sign "Paul Jaboulet Ainé".
The swimming pool is fed from a spring & cold, but clean & chlorine-free. One of the nicest swimming pools to swim in.
The boys have been playing Irish tin whistles to drown out the Belgians' 60's pop music from next door.
we caught the 10.15 ferry & crossed in warm sunshine.
